LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S MOTION TO SEAL PERTAINING TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS. GRANT. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM MURRAY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Specially appearing defendant William Murray's motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted.

Plaintiff alleges that in January 2020, in Oahu, Hawaii, she was sexually assaulted by defendant and cross-defendant Murray, an Uber driver. Murray (originally sued as Doe One) is a resident of Honolulu, Hawaii; has never lived or intended to establish residence in California; has no business or property interests and conducts no business activities in the State; and has only driven for Uber in Hawaii. (Murray Decl. 2-5, 7.)

"[W]hen jurisdiction is challenged by a nonresident defendant, the burden of proof is upon the plaintiff to demonstrate that 'minimum contacts' exist between defendant and the forum state to justify imposition of personal jurisdiction." (Mihlon v. Superior Court (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) Plaintiff does not meet its burden to show that Mr. Murray is subject to personal jurisdiction in California. Plaintiff's central argument is that Mr. Murray is subject to specific jurisdiction because he entered into a contract to work with Uber, a California company. (Plf.'s Opp., 3:13-15.) It is settled law, however, that an individual employee is not subject to personal jurisdiction based on his employer's contacts. An individual's "contacts with California are not to be judged according to [his] employer's activities there . . . Each defendant's contacts with the forum State must be assessed individually." (Calder v. Jones (1984) 465 U.S. 783, 790." The "relationship must arise out of contacts that the defendant himself creates with the forum State," not by "contacts between the plaintiff (or third parties) and the forum State." (Walden v. Fiore (2014) 571 U.S. 277, 284; see also Keeton v. Hustler Magazine, Inc. (1984) 465 U.S. 770, 781, fn. 13 ["[J]urisdiction over an employee does not automatically follow from jurisdiction over the corporation which employs him . . . ."].) Further, plaintiff's claims do not arise out of or relate to Murray's contacts with the forum. (Bristol-Myers Squibb Co. v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 137 S.Ct. 1773, 1780 [specific jurisdiction is "confined to adjudication of issues deriving from, or connected with, the very controversy that establishes jurisdiction"].) As Murray points out, if plaintiff's reasoning were accepted, any Uber driver anywhere in the world would be subject to suit in California.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. WALT CUBBERLY, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby, LLP (Tel: 713-230-2200) and JESSICA DAYTON, Esq. of ADZ Law, LLP (Tel: 650-458-2300) for plaintiff;
Attys. GARY A. WOLENSKY, Esq. and ROBERT ATKINS, Esq. of Buchalter (Tel: 949-760-1121) for Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RAISIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's "allegations for the purpose of prejudicing Uber" are colorful, but within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for punitive damages is pled with insufficient particularity, that part of the motion is granted with leave to amend. Presumably plaintiff will be amending her complaint to plead fraud with greater particularity, and fraud plays a prominent role in punitive damages law. (See, e.g., CACI 3946.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. WALT CUBBERLY, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby, LLP (Tel: 713-230-2200) and JESSICA DAYTON, Esq. of ADZ Law, LLP (Tel: 650-458-2300) for plaintiff;
Attys. GARY A. WOLENSKY, Esq. and ROBERT ATKINS, Esq. of Buchalter (Tel: 949-760-1121) for Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RAISIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's demurrers to six of the complaint's numbered causes of action are sustained or overruled as follows:
4 (fraud). Plaintiff fails to plead fraud with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.
5-8 (assault, sexual battery, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress). Defendants' demurrer ignores the complaint's allegations that "Uber is a common carrier" and thus liable for assaults "regardless of whether such acts were committed within the course and scope of employment for Uber." (Cmpl., 17:4-11.) These allegations may or may not prove factually founded, but that is not for resolution at the pleading state. This demurrer is overruled.
9 (Ralph Act). The complaint adequately pled the elements of a Ralph Act claim as to driver Doe One. (Cmplt., 28:21-29:18; CACI 3063.) Plaintiff's opposition suggests she can also plead theories of Ralph Act liability against the demurring defendants, but has yet to do so. This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Seal Pertaining To Pltf'S Opposition To Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (through Zoom):
Walt Cubberly, Esq. 650-458-2300 of ADZ Law for Plaintiff.
Steven Disharoon, Esq. 707-559-6701 SDisharoon@wshblaw.com for Defendant Uber.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JANE DOE's Motion To Seal Pertaining To Plaintiff's Opposition To Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens.

Grant. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
